rules.risk: Anonymous authentication allows unauthenticated requests to the API server. An attacker on the network can query or modify cluster resources without any credentials.
rules.remediation: Set --anonymous-auth=false on the kube-apiserver command line.

rules.risk: The insecure port serves the API over HTTP without authentication or authorization. Any process on the network can perform any API operation with full cluster-admin privileges.
rules.remediation: Set --insecure-port=0 on the kube-apiserver command line. This flag is deprecated since Kubernetes 1.20 and removed in 1.24.

rules.description: kube-apiserver authorization mode does not include RBAC
rules.risk: Without RBAC in the authorization chain, the cluster cannot enforce fine-grained access control. Modes like AlwaysAllow grant every authenticated request full permissions.
Without an encryption provider configuration, Kubernetes secrets and other sensitive resources are stored unencrypted in etcd. Anyone with access to etcd can read all secrets in plaintext.
rules.remediation: Configure --encryption-provider-config to point to an EncryptionConfiguration file that encrypts secrets at rest.

rules.description: kube-apiserver has no admission plugins configured
rules.risk: Without admission plugins, the API server cannot enforce policies on incoming requests. Security-critical plugins like PodSecurity, NodeRestriction, and ServiceAccount will not be active.

rules.description: etcd client TLS certificate is not configured
rules.risk: Without client TLS, etcd serves its API over unencrypted HTTP. An attacker on the network can read and modify all cluster state including secrets, tokens, and configuration.
rules.remediation: Set --cert-file and --key-file on the etcd command line to enable client TLS.

rules.description: etcd client TLS key is not configured
rules.risk: Without the client TLS key, TLS cannot be properly established for etcd client connections. This leaves the etcd API unencrypted and vulnerable to eavesdropping.
rules.remediation: Set --key-file on the etcd command line alongside --cert-file.

rules.description: etcd peer TLS certificate is not configured
rules.risk: Without peer TLS, etcd cluster members communicate over unencrypted connections. An attacker can impersonate an etcd peer, inject corrupted data, or eavesdrop on all replicated state.
rules.remediation: Set --peer-cert-file and --peer-key-file on the etcd command line to enable peer TLS.

rules.description: etcd peer TLS key is not configured
rules.risk: Without the peer TLS key, peer-to-peer TLS cannot be established. etcd members replicate data without encryption, exposing secrets during transit between nodes.
rules.remediation: Set --peer-key-file on the etcd command line alongside --peer-cert-file.

rules.description: etcd auto-TLS is enabled for client connections
rules.risk: Auto-TLS generates self-signed certificates that are not verifiable by clients. This defeats certificate-based authentication and allows any client to connect to etcd without presenting a valid certificate.
rules.remediation: Set --auto-tls=false and configure proper CA-signed certificates with --cert-file and --key-file.

rules.description: kube-controller-manager service account private key is not configured
rules.risk: Without --service-account-private-key-file, the controller manager cannot sign service account tokens. Pods relying on projected tokens may fail authentication, and token rotation is ineffective.
rules.remediation: Set --service-account-private-key-file to the path of the service account signing key.

rules.description: kube-controller-manager root CA file is not configured
rules.risk: Without --root-ca-file, service account tokens are not validated against the cluster CA. Pods cannot verify the API server certificate, enabling man-in-the-middle attacks on in-cluster communication.
rules.remediation: Set --root-ca-file to the path of the cluster root CA certificate.

rules.description: kube-controller-manager does not use individual service account credentials
rules.risk: Without --use-service-account-credentials=true, all controllers run with the controller manager's own credentials instead of individual service accounts. This violates least-privilege and makes it harder to audit controller actions.
rules.remediation: Set --use-service-account-credentials=true on the kube-controller-manager command line.

rules.description: kube-apiserver audit logging path is not configured
rules.risk: Without --audit-log-path, the API server does not write audit logs to disk. Security-relevant events such as secret access, RBAC changes, and pod creation are not recorded, hindering incident response and forensics.
rules.remediation: Set --audit-log-path to a persistent file path (e.g., /var/log/kubernetes/audit.log) and configure --audit-log-maxage, --audit-log-maxsize, --audit-log-maxbackup.

rules.description: kube-apiserver audit policy file is not configured
rules.risk: Without --audit-policy-file, the API server has no audit policy and will not generate audit events even if --audit-log-path is set. No audit trail means compromises go undetected.
rules.remediation: Create an audit policy file and set --audit-policy-file on the kube-apiserver command line. At minimum, log Metadata level for all resources.

rules.description: API server audit logging is enabled but retention policy is not configured
rules.risk: Without audit-log-maxage and audit-log-maxbackup, audit logs may grow unbounded (disk exhaustion) or be missing retention guarantees for compliance.
Profiling endpoints expose runtime performance data that can reveal scheduling decisions and resource utilization patterns, aiding targeted attacks against cluster workloads.
rules.remediation: Set --profiling=false on the kube-scheduler command line.

rules.description: kube-scheduler is bound to a non-localhost address
rules.risk: Binding the scheduler to a non-localhost address exposes its endpoints to the network, potentially allowing unauthorized access to scheduling metrics and health checks.
rules.remediation: Set --bind-address=127.0.0.1 on the kube-scheduler command line.
